body.input: User was getting workspace protocol errors and build failures with npm/pnpm confusion. Requested to simplify down the build process and make it completely robust with one-touch setup.
actions: Created pnpm-workspace.yaml for proper workspace configuration
Standardized all package.json scripts to use pnpm instead of npm
Added .npmrc with pnpm configuration (auto-install-peers, ignore-scripts:false)
Updated setup.sh script to auto-install pnpm and use it for all operations
Updated README with clear one-command setup and pnpm usage examples
Added packageManager field to enforce pnpm@10.27.0
Changed build script to sequential execution to handle dependency order

files.why: Required for pnpm workspace configuration
Configure pnpm behavior for native deps
Standardized all scripts to pnpm, added packageManager field
Auto-install pnpm, use it for all operations
Document pnpm usage and one-command setup

decisions.choice: Use pnpm exclusively instead of npm/yarn mix
decisions.reason: Eliminates workspace: protocol errors and provides better monorepo support

decisions.choice: Sequential build order instead of parallel
decisions.reason: Core must build first to generate types for server/tui/chat-web
Reduces friction - users only need Node.js installed

decisions.choice: Add preinstall hook to block npm
decisions.reason: Prevents accidental use of wrong package manager

lessons: The workspace: protocol is pnpm/yarn-specific and causes EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL errors with npm
pnpm requires pnpm-workspace.yaml file, not package.json workspaces field
Native dependencies (better-sqlite3, esbuild) need build script approval in pnpm v10+
Build order matters in monorepos - core package must build before dependents
TypeScript declaration files may not emit if tsconfig inheritance is misconfigured

front.title: Comprehensive migration from pnpm to Bun

body.input: User requested comprehensive and robust migration to Bun with everything working completely and fully, same one-touch setup on new machines.
actions: Researched Bun workspace and monorepo capabilities (2026 improvements)
Installed Bun runtime (v1.3.5)
Removed pnpm-specific files (.npmrc, pnpm-workspace.yaml, pnpm-lock.yaml)
Updated package.json with Bun packageManager and workspace config
Replaced all pnpm commands with bun equivalents in scripts
Created scripts/build-workspace.sh for robust sequential builds
Updated setup.sh to auto-install Bun from official installer
Documented performance benefits and migration guide
Tested clean install and build process
Created comprehensive MIGRATION.md documentation

files.why: Updated packageManager to bun, added workspaces array, converted all scripts to use bun
Auto-install Bun via curl, use bun commands throughout
Robust build script that handles dependency order with PATH management
Updated all documentation to reference Bun, added performance benefits
Comprehensive migration guide with commands, known issues, rollback
Fixed build script to create dist/providers directory before copy
No longer needed - Bun doesn't use npm config
Replaced by workspaces array in package.json
Replaced by bun.lockb binary lockfile

decisions.reason: 70% faster installs, native TypeScript, built-in SQLite 3-6x faster, better DX
Maintains Node.js compatibility, can migrate to bun:sqlite later
Hook fails during Bun install due to PATH issues, documentation makes Bun obvious choice
Bun's --filter flag behavior differs from pnpm, explicit script more reliable
Respects dependency order: core must build before server/tui/chat-web

lessons: Bun v1.3+ (2026) has mature workspace support competitive with pnpm/yarn
Bun's --filter flag syntax differs from pnpm, may need wrapper scripts
Bun has built-in SQLite via bun:sqlite that's 3-6x faster than better-sqlite3
Native dependency builds work automatically with Bun (esbuild, etc)
Bun install is 70% faster than pnpm, 3x faster than npm in real-world tests
TypeScript declaration issues are pre-existing codebase problems, not Bun-related
One-touch setup requires PATH management in build scripts (HOME/.bun/bin)
Bun's lockfile (bun.lockb) is binary format, faster and more reliable
Preinstall hooks need careful PATH handling or should be avoided with Bun

body.input: User requested to ultrathink and fix all TypeScript declaration file issues preventing packages from finding types from @tron/core and other workspace packages.
actions: Diagnosed root cause: path mappings pointing to src instead of dist
Removed path mappings from root tsconfig.json (use Node module resolution)
Fixed tsconfig.build.json in core to explicitly set declaration flags
Added project references to server and tui tsconfigs
Discovered stale .tsbuildinfo files preventing declaration emission
Cleaned all tsbuildinfo and verified declarations generate correctly
Tested full clean build - all packages now have type declarations
Verified 29 total .d.ts files across core/server/tui packages

files.why: Removed path mappings - let Node resolve workspace packages
Added explicit declaration, declarationMap, composite flags
Added declaration flags and project reference to core
Added declaration flags and references to core+server

decisions.reason: Path mappings pointed to src/ during build, preventing TypeScript from finding .d.ts in dist/. Node's module resolution through package.json exports is more reliable for workspaces.
Incremental compilation was using cached state that prevented new declaration files from being emitted. Fresh build resolved it.

decisions.choice: Add explicit declaration flags to all tsconfigs
decisions.reason: Even though root tsconfig had declaration:true, child configs need explicit flags to override properly.
TypeScript composite projects need references for proper incremental builds and type checking across packages.

lessons: TypeScript path mappings in monorepos cause issues when src/ and dist/ differ
Stale .tsbuildinfo files can prevent declaration emission even when config is correct
Each workspace package needs explicit declaration:true, not just inheritance
Project references are essential for TypeScript workspace type checking
'tsc --listEmittedFiles' is invaluable for debugging why files aren't generating
Clean the entire build (including tsbuildinfo) when config changes significantly
Node module resolution via package.json exports is more reliable than paths
TypeScript composite projects require declaration:true to work properly

front.title: Complete Bun migration with Python 3.11 fix for transcription

body.input: Migrate project from pnpm to Bun, fix all build issues, ensure one-touch setup works
actions: Migrated package manager from pnpm to Bun 1.3.5
Removed pnpm-workspace.yaml and .npmrc files
Created scripts/build-workspace.sh for sequential builds
Fixed TypeScript declaration generation by removing path mappings and adding explicit flags
Added @tron/core dependency to chat-web package
Fixed Vite config for workspace package resolution
Fixed transcription-sidecar to prefer Python 3.11/3.12 for ML compatibility
Updated all package.json scripts to use Bun syntax (cd packages/X && bun run Y)
Merged with upstream changes and resolved pnpm-workspace.yaml conflict

files.why: Bun workspaces config and scripts
Sequential build for workspace packages
Auto-install Bun
Prefer Python 3.11/3.12 for ML packages
Workspace package resolution
Replaced by package.json workspaces

decisions.choice: Use cd && bun run pattern instead of bun --filter

decisions.reason: ML packages like onnxruntime lack wheels for Python 3.13+

lessons: Bun supports workspace:* protocol but --filter syntax differs from pnpm
Python 3.13+ lacks wheels for many ML packages - prefer 3.11/3.12
Always clean tsbuildinfo files when changing TypeScript configs
Vite needs explicit alias config to resolve monorepo workspace packages

front.title: Bun migration - merged with upstream browser fixes

body.input: Stash Bun migration, pull upstream changes (browser fixes, iOS updates), unstash and verify everything works
actions: Pulled upstream changes: BROWSER_FIXES.md, browser-service changes, iOS BrowserSheetView, event-store-orchestrator updates, pnpm-lock.yaml updates
Resolved Xcode UserInterfaceState.xcuserstate conflict (accepted upstream)
Deleted upstream pnpm-lock.yaml and pnpm-workspace.yaml (replaced by Bun)
Clean install with Bun (13s, 1026 packages)
Full build successful (all 4 packages)
All 1900 tests pass (1463 vitest + 437 chat-web)

files.why: Replaced by bun.lock
Bun uses package.json workspaces

decisions.reason: Binary UI state file - upstream version more recent

lessons: When merging Bun migration with upstream, always delete any new pnpm files that upstream adds
Xcode xcuserstate files are binary - always accept one version rather than trying to merge

front.title: Fix context.compact RPC method not found error

body.input: iOS app shows 'Unknown method: context.compact' error when tapping Compact button
actions: Added context.compact to RpcMethod type union
Added context.compact case in handler switch that delegates to handleContextConfirmCompaction

files.why: Add context.compact to RpcMethod type
Add context.compact handler case

decisions.reason: iOS params (sessionId) are compatible with confirmCompaction which only requires sessionId (editedSummary optional). Result types compatible as Swift ignores extra fields.

lessons: When iOS RPC method not found, check server handler.ts switch statement for missing case
context.compact is an alias for context.confirmCompaction - one-step compaction vs three-step flow

front.title: Fix Invalid request error on session resume with agentic loops

body.input: iOS app fails to send message after attempting compact - Invalid request error from Anthropic API
actions: Diagnosed root cause: tool.result events not being reconstructed as user messages during session resume
Fixed event-store.ts getMessagesAt() to accumulate tool.result events and flush them as user messages before next assistant message
This ensures proper message alternation: assistant(tool_use) -> user(tool_result) -> assistant(response)

files.why: Reconstruct tool.result events as user messages to fix consecutive assistant message issue

decisions.choice: Reconstruct tool_result from tool.result events during message building
decisions.reason: event-store-orchestrator.ts intentionally does NOT persist tool results as message.user at turn end (line 2389). The fix synthesizes them during reconstruction instead.

lessons: When Anthropic API returns Invalid request after session resume, check for consecutive same-role messages
tool.result events must be converted to user messages with tool_result content blocks for proper API format
The event store comment at line 416 was incorrect - tool results were NOT being stored as message.user events
